word: H6117 - עָקַב - ʻâqab - aw-kab' - Take by the heel

sections.paragraph:
Original: עקב

Transliteration: ‛âqab

Phonetic: aw-kab'

BDB Definition:

  1. to supplant, circumvent, take by the heel, follow at the heel, assail insidiously, overreach
    1. (Qal) to supplant, overreach, attack at the heel
    2. (Piel) to hold back

Origin: a primitive root

TWOT entry: 1676

Part(s) of speech: Verb
